message:
perf(server/tui): add contrast guard cache, optimize event ring, and overhaul reservation polling

Add ContrastGuardCache to memoize per-frame color contrast computations
(readable_fg, decorative_fg, minimum ratios, contrast ratios) across
the ~width*height cell iterations of apply_frame_contrast_guard. This
eliminates redundant sRGB luminance math for repeated background colors.

Add recent_event_signals() to EventRingBuffer that returns lightweight
(timestamp, severity) tuples instead of cloning full MailEvent payloads.
The ambient health input path only needs timing and severity, so this
avoids copying event bodies/metadata every frame.

Refactor EventRingBuffer internal iteration into shared helper methods
(collect_recent_from_back, collect_events_since_seq) and optimize
events_since_seq to scan backwards from the tail so steady-state
polling is O(new_events) rather than O(capacity).

Overhaul TUI reservation polling with dual-mode scan strategy:
- ActiveFast mode uses SQL WHERE predicates when the schema stores
  expires_ts as INTEGER (the modern path)
- FullLegacy mode scans all rows with Rust-side filtering for databases
  that still have TEXT timestamp columns from older schemas
- Mode is auto-detected via PRAGMA table_info + typeof() probe, cached
  per DB path for 5 minutes
- Reservation counts are computed during the scan pass and reused for
  both the aggregate counter and per-project project list, eliminating
  redundant queries

Use BinaryHeap for top-K reservation selection (capped at
MAX_RESERVATIONS=1000) to avoid sorting the full result set.

Add stable ordering tiebreakers (id DESC) to agent, project, and
contact list queries to prevent non-deterministic row shuffling across
poll cycles. Add MIN_POLL_INTERVAL (100ms) floor to prevent accidental
busy-loops from CONSOLE_POLL_INTERVAL_MS=0.

Clean up expired entries from reservation_tracker in tui_app to prevent
unbounded map growth across long-running sessions.

Hoist config_snapshot().tui_effects read above the ambient render call
to avoid borrowing self.state inside the frame closure after it has
already been partially borrowed. Pass effects_enabled as a parameter to
ambient_mode_for_frame (now const fn).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
